Sales people build value. They don't discount price. When they get a price ejection they don't think, oh let me lower the price. They think, oh I need to build the value. See, see here's what I could do. I can give them four dollars. It's a four million dollar transaction. So how about I give you, here I got four dollars on me right now. I'm packing four dollars. Okay, we'll take it from four million down to three million, whatever the number is, right? We'll take it down four dollars. Does that do it? How about four hundred thousand? How about, how about we just discount it to zero? Would you make a decision? See that's not what great sales people do. Great sales people, average sales people, unprepared sales people, untrained sales people, and sales managers discount price. You know what great people do? Build value. They build value. They solve problems. They don't give money away. Look if people didn't want to spend money they wouldn't have sat down with you. People want to get something. Great sales people know that. They have completely disconnected this concept of price, discounting price as a way to get somebody to make a decision. They think about this. Let me build value. Let me add value. Let me show them why this four million is worth eight million. Let me show them why this four million dollar product is going to save them four hundred grand a month. Let me show them why this product and maybe the great sales people actually even just disconnect on this concept of discounting by saying something like this. Sir, I don't have any more money to give you. The only way we're going to figure this out is if I build value. And when value exceeds price, sir, price is no longer the issue and we'll do this deal.
